chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.S-272 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For orders on M.A No.4449/2025. 3. For hearing of main case. 19.02.2026 Mr. Vicky Kumar Khatri, Advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Shareef Solangi, Assistant A.G Sindh. = The case of the petitioners is that a compromise order dated 22.12.2022 was passed on the basis of statements between the petitioner and defendant No.1 in the suit, which was challenged through an application under Section 12 C.P.C filed by respondent No.11. The said application was allowed and evidence was recorded thereon. Such order was concurrently upheld by the learned Appellate Court, which has been assailed in the instant petition. During course of hearing, learned counsel has informed that the trial of the main suit has proceeded, and the same is at the stage of recording of evidence of the Plaintiff’s side. After arguing the matter at length, learned counsel states that he would be satisfied if the instant petition is disposed of in the terms that the learned trial court be directed firstly that while deciding of the suit, not to get influenced by the observations made in the impugned orders while deciding the application under Section 12 C.P.C; and secondly, that clear direction for expeditious disposal of the trial be issued to the learned trial court. 2 In view of the above, the instant petition is disposed of in the terms, that the learned trial court is directed to conclude the trial within a period of 60 days from the date of receipt of this order and shall decide the matter on the basis of the evidence produced by the parties, without granting any unnecessary adjournments to the parties and without being influenced by the observations made by the learned Courts below while deciding the application under Section 12 C.P.C in the impugned orders.

During course of hearing, learned counsel has informed that the trial of the main suit has proceeded, and the same is at the stage of recording of evidence of the Plaintiff’s side. After arguing the matter at length, learned counsel states that he would be satisfied if the instant petition is disposed of in the terms that the learned trial court be directed firstly that while deciding of the suit, not to get influenced by the observations made in the impugned orders while deciding the application under Section 12 C.P.C; and secondly, that clear direction for expeditious disposal of the trial be issued to the learned trial court. 2 In view of the above, the instant petition is disposed of in the terms, that the learned trial court is directed to conclude the trial within a period of 60 days from the date of receipt of this order and shall decide the matter on the basis of the evidence produced by the parties, without granting any unnecessary adjournments to the parties and without being influenced by the observations made by the learned Courts below while deciding the application under Section 12 C.P.C in the impugned orders. In compliance, the learned trial court shall send a copy of the judgment to the learned Additional Registrar of this Court. The petition stands disposed of alongwith listed application in above terms. JUDGE *Faisal*
